about.title: About
about.subtitle: The Story Behind the Music
about.bio_paragraph_1: Alexandros Tsopozidis was born on January 1, 1986, in the village of Sameba (Guniakala), Georgia. Of Pontic Greek heritage, he grew up surrounded by the rich musical traditions of the Caucasus region, where Eastern melodies intertwined with Greek folk rhythms.
about.bio_paragraph_2: In 1998, at the age of 12, Alexandros moved with his family to Paphos, Cyprus, where he began a new chapter of his life. Initially drawn to athletics, he trained at a Cyprus football academy with dreams of a professional sports career.
about.bio_paragraph_3: At his parents' request, Alexandros left football behind and discovered his true calling — music. In 2011, he began his musical journey in earnest, and his talent quickly caught the attention of Russian producer Kemran Amirov, who helped launch his career.
about.bio_paragraph_4: His debut duet with Eldar Dalgatov, 'Может ты вернёшься,' introduced him to Russian-speaking audiences. But it was his collaboration with Elbrus Dzhanmirzoev on 'Бродяга' that catapulted him to fame, amassing over 22 million views on YouTube.
about.bio_paragraph_5: Today, Alexandros performs in Russian, Greek, and Pontic Greek, blending pop with Caucasian and Eastern influences into a unique sound. Based in Paphos, Cyprus, he continues to release new music, with his latest single 'Канитель' (2026) and 'Вечная любовь' coming soon. He is the recipient of the 9 Волна award for his contribution to ethnic music.
about.today_text: Based in Paphos, Cyprus. Latest release: "Канитель" (2026). "Вечная любовь" coming soon.

press.title: Press Kit
press.subtitle: For media, event organizers, and booking agents
press.meta_description: Alexandros Tsopozidis press kit — bio, photos, booking info for media, wedding planners, and event organizers. Book for weddings, christenings & private events.
press.fact_genre: Greek-Eastern Pop, Caucasian Music, Pontic Greek
press.short_bio: Alexandros Tsopozidis is a Pontic Greek vocalist bridging the Caucasus, the Mediterranean, and the global stage. Singing in Russian, Greek, and Pontic Greek, he fuses Eastern melodies with contemporary pop — a sound that earned him 22 million YouTube views and the prestigious 9 Волна award for ethnic music.
press.medium_bio: Born in the village of Sameba, Georgia, and raised in Paphos, Cyprus, Alexandros Tsopozidis carries the soul of the Caucasus in every note. A Pontic Greek artist performing in three languages — Russian, Greek, and Pontic Greek — he crafts a signature sound where Eastern tradition meets modern pop. His breakout collaboration "Бродяга" with Elbrus Dzhanmirzoev surpassed 22 million YouTube views, while hits like "Male Male" (11M+ views) cemented his reputation across the Russian-speaking world. A 9 Волна award laureate, Alexandros commands festival stages from Moscow to Baku. Latest releases: "Канитель" (2026), with "Вечная любовь" arriving soon.
press.full_bio:
Alexandros Tsopozidis is a genre-defying vocalist whose music bridges the ancient traditions of the Caucasus with the pulse of contemporary pop. Born on January 1, 1986, in the mountain village of Sameba (Guniakala), Georgia, he grew up steeped in the Pontic Greek musical heritage of his ancestors — a world where Eastern melodies and Greek folk rhythms exist as one.

At twelve, his family relocated to Paphos, Cyprus, where young Alexandros initially chased a career in professional football. That athletic discipline would later shape his relentless work ethic on stage. When he pivoted to music in 2011, Russian producer Kemran Amirov recognized his raw talent immediately, launching a career that would span continents and cultures.

His debut duet with Eldar Dalgatov, "Может ты вернёшься," introduced him to the Russian-speaking world. But it was "Бродяга," his collaboration with Elbrus Dzhanmirzoev, that became a cultural phenomenon — surpassing 22 million YouTube views and establishing Alexandros as one of the most distinctive voices in Eurasian pop. Subsequent hits including "Male Male" (11M+ views), "Армяне и греки — братья навеки," and album "За тобой" reinforced his unique position at the crossroads of Greek, Caucasian, and Eastern European music.

A recipient of the 9 Волна award for contribution to ethnic music, Alexandros has headlined major festivals including Жара (Baku), Карнавала (Gelendzhik), Кавказ Мьюзик Фест, and "Звёзды Востока" with Восток FM in Moscow.

Performing in Russian, Greek, and Pontic Greek, he continues to release music that honors tradition while pushing boundaries. His latest single "Канитель" (2026) is out now, with "Вечная любовь" arriving soon. Based in Paphos, Cyprus.
